session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9361 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the pr-feedback-classifier skill and execute it with the specified PR number.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Classify PR review feedback by filling in semantic fields from mechanical CLI output. 16→ 17→## Steps 18→ 19→1. **Run mechanical classification:** 20→ 21→ ```bash 22→ erk exec classify-pr-feedback [--pr <number>] [--include-resolved] 23→ ``` 24→ 25→ Pass `--pr <number>` if specified in `$ARGUMENTS`. Pass `--include-resolved` if specified in `$ARGUMENTS`. 26→ 27→ This returns JSON with mechanically pre-classified items: 28→ - **review_submissions**: PR-level reviews (classification: "actionable" | "informational" | "needs_llm") 29→ - **review_threads**: Inline review threads (pre_existing_candidate flag set for bot + restructured) 30→ - **discussion_comments**: Discussion comments (classification: "informational" | "needs_llm")
... [169 lines omitted] ...
104→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 106→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 144→ "error": null 159→## Error Case 173→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 191→ "error": null

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run mechanical PR feedback classification for PR 9361

## ✅ Tripwires Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-21 09:04:11 PT

No violations detected. Code properly implements new gateway method across all 4 required locations, includes comprehensive tests, follows all erk coding standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Tripwires Triggered
- Gateway ABC method addition → loaded universal-tripwires.md, architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md
- 4-place gateway implementation pattern → loaded architecture/tripwires.md
- Subprocess in real.py → loaded architecture/tripwires.md

### Tier 1 Pattern Matches (Mechanical)
✅ `subprocess\.run\(` - None found (uses execute_gh_command_with_retry wrapper)
✅ `gt\s+` commands - None found
✅ `Path\.home\(\)` - None found
✅ `import time` / `time\.sleep` / `datetime\.now` - None found
✅ `os\.chdir\(\)` - None found
✅ `monkeypatch` / `@patch` - None found in new test code

### Tier 2 Semantic Matches (LLM-Derived)
✅ Adding new gateway method → Properly implemented in 4 places (abc.py, real.py, dry_run.py, fake.py)
✅ Real gateway methods with subprocess calls → Comprehensive unit tests included (test_reopen_contested_threads.py)
✅ Exec command registration → Properly added to group.py and reference.md
✅ Frozen dataclasses → All new dataclasses properly use @dataclass(frozen=True)
✅ Test mutation tracking → Fake properly exposes unresolved_thread_ids property

<sub>Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-03-21 04:02 PM UTC</sub>","createdAt":"2026-03-21T16:02:12Z","id":"IC_kwDOPxC3hc70mFxR","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9361#issuecomment-4103625809","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"author":{"login":"github-actions"},"authorAssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"<!-- erk:pr-address-run -->
## PR Review Comments Addressed

This PR was updated by an automated process triggered by:
```
erk pr address-remote 9361
```

### What is this?

[Erk](https://github.com/schrockn/erk) is a CLI tool for plan-oriented agentic
engineering. The `pr-address` workflow uses Claude to read PR review comments
and address them automatically.

### Changes Made

The logic for checking if a PR review thread is unresolved was simplified from a double-negative comparison (`is False`) to a direct negation (`not`), making the code more idiomatic and easier to read without changing its behavior.

### Details

- **Model:** claude-sonnet-4-6
- **Workflow run:** [View details](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/23383299189)
- **Commits added:** 1

---
*Automated by erk pr-address workflow*","createdAt":"2026-03-21T16:03:29Z","id":"IC_kwDOPxC3hc70mGWy","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9361#issuecomment-4103628210","viewerDidAuthor":true}] [{"author":{"login":"github-actions"},"authorAssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"","commit":{"oid":"2b5b5892fd282b9b9c37dfe816ded9459b4e0f82"},"id":"PRR_kwDOPxC3hc7tmAqq","includesCreatedEdit":false,"reactionGroups":[],"state":"COMMENTED","submittedAt":"2026-03-21T15:41:35Z"},{"author":{"login":"github-actions"},"authorAssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"","commit":{"oid":"2b5b5892fd282b9b9c37dfe816ded9459b4e0f82"},"id":"PRR_kwDOPxC3hc7tmFPw","includesCreatedEdit":false,"reactionGroups":[],"state":"COMMENTED","submittedAt":"2026-03-21T16:02:03Z"},{"author":{"login":"github-actions"},"authorAssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"","commit":{"oid":"897d8eca39ae13a137e7f9160c7df6a01fed7b44"},"id":"PRR_kwDOPxC3hc7tmF8j","includesCreatedEdit":false,"reactionGroups":[],"state":"COMMENTED","submittedAt":"2026-03-21T16:04:30Z"}]

tool_use.param: gh pr view 9361 --json reviewThreads --jq '.reviewThreads[] | select(.line != null)' 2>/dev/null

tool_use.param: gh pr comments 9361 --json author,authorAssociation,body,url 2>/dev/null | jq '.[] | select(.author.login == "github-actions" and (.body | contains("Unnecessary list")))' | head -100
Find the detailed inline comment about list conversion

session.assistant: Let me check the actual code file to understand the violation:
session.tool_result:
70→ Args: 71→ threads: All review threads (resolved and unresolved) 72→ 73→ Returns: 74→ List of contested threads (resolved, had marker, have post-marker comments) 75→ """ 76→ contested: list[PRReviewThread] = [] 77→ for thread in threads: 78→ if not thread.is_resolved: 79→ continue 80→ 81→ comments = list(thread.comments) 82→ 83→ # Find the index of the last marker comment 84→ last_marker_index: int | None = None 85→ for i, comment in enumerate(comments): 86→ if _has_marker(comment.body): 87→ last_marker_index = i 88→ 89→ if last_marker_index is None: 90→ # No marker — this was manually resolved, not by pr-address 91→ continue 92→ 93→ # Check if any comments exist after the last marker 94→ if last_marker_index < len(comments) - 1:

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>
